title: PipeShare user-prefs sync storm fix (dbg131)
description:
PipeShare was firing many PATCH /me/user-prefs requests in quick succession (~1s each), making the UI feel laggy on load and theme changes. The root cause was a feedback loop: each prefs sync dispatched hp-user-prefs-synced, theme init re-applied the theme and wrote prefs back to the server even when nothing changed, and the sync layer had no in-flight guard or minimum interval between patches.

This release debounces and coalesces user-prefs writes (3s debounce, 2.5s min interval, single in-flight PATCH), skips set() when values are unchanged, and makes theme init paint-only so server sync events do not re-trigger PATCH. DAS monitor-snapshot polling is slightly slower (20s visible) to reduce background API noise.

Who is affected: OVH private server on deploy. SaaS when Mike applies the platform version in releases.html.
changeLog: Fix user-prefs sync storm: debounce, throttle, coalesce in-flight PATCH.
Skip PATCH when pref value unchanged; break theme ↔ sync feedback loop.
Theme init paints DOM only; user theme changes still sync once.
Cache-bust user-prefs-sync.js and theme-profile.js on PipeShare portal.
DAS monitor-snapshot poll 15s → 20s when tab visible.
